rejectionReason: Your app includes user-generated content but does not include the required moderation controls.
whatDeveloperDid: Built an education app (FlipLearn) with a social feed where students could post content, but did not include any report, block, or flag buttons on posts or user profiles.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.2 - Safety - User-Generated Content: Your app enables the display of user-generated content but does not have the required precautions in place. Specifically, your app is missing the ability to report objectionable content, block abusive users, and a mechanism to flag content for review.
fix: Added a flag/report button on every post and user profile, implemented user blocking functionality, added a 24-hour content moderation SLA with a dedicated moderation queue, and included a visible link to community guidelines.

rejectionReason: Your Kids Category app includes third-party analytics with the ability to collect identifiable information from minors.
whatDeveloperDid: Published a kids educational game in the Kids Category on the App Store while including Firebase Analytics SDK, which can collect device identifiers and behavioral data in violation of COPPA regulations.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.3 - Safety - Kids Category: Your app includes third-party analytics software with the ability to collect and transmit identifiable information about children. Specifically, we found your app integrates Firebase Analytics which may collect device identifiers. Apps in the Kids Category must not include third-party analytics or advertising that can collect identifiable information.
fix: Removed Firebase Analytics SDK entirely from the app. Replaced with a custom first-party analytics solution that collects only anonymous aggregate event counts stored on the developer's own server, with no device identifiers or user-level tracking.

rejectionReason: Your Kids Category app includes third-party analytics (Amplitude) which may collect identifiable information from children.
whatDeveloperDid: Integrated Amplitude analytics SDK in a children's learning app listed in the Kids Category to track user engagement and session metrics.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.3 - Safety - Kids Category: We found that your app uses the Amplitude SDK, which has the capability to collect identifiable information. Apps in the Kids Category may not include third-party analytics or third-party advertising. You must remove all such SDKs before resubmitting.
fix: Removed Amplitude SDK completely. Built a lightweight custom event tracker that only logs anonymized session durations and feature usage counts without any device fingerprinting, IDFA, or persistent identifiers.
behavioralSignals: third-party analytics present in Kids Category app

rejectionReason: Your app connects users anonymously for video chat, which presents a high risk of pornographic or objectionable content.
whatDeveloperDid: Built a Chatroulette-style anonymous video chat app that randomly paired strangers for live video conversations with no content filtering or identity verification.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.1.4 - Safety - Objectionable Content: Your app facilitates anonymous video connections between users, which creates significant risk for pornographic content, exploitation, and abuse. Apps that connect strangers for anonymous communication must implement robust safeguards including content moderation and age verification.
fix: The developer was unable to adequately address Apple's concerns. Anonymous random video chat apps are effectively banned from the App Store due to the inherent risk of objectionable content. The developer pivoted to a moderated, interest-based matching system with AI-powered nudity detection, mandatory profile verification, and real-time content monitoring.

rejectionReason: Your app provides drug dosage calculations but is not from a recognized institution such as a drug manufacturer, hospital, or university.
whatDeveloperDid: An independent developer built a drug dosage calculator app for healthcare professionals that calculated dosages based on patient weight, age, and renal function without affiliation to any medical institution.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.4.2 - Safety - Medical: Your app provides drug dosage calculations. Per App Store Review Guidelines, drug dosage calculators must come from the drug manufacturer, a hospital, university, health insurance company, pharmacy, or other approved entity. We are unable to accept drug dosage calculators from independent developers due to the potential risk to patient safety.
fix: The developer partnered with a university hospital's pharmacy department, which agreed to co-publish the app under the hospital's developer account with clinical pharmacist review and validation of all dosage formulas.

rejectionReason: Your app contains content that requires a higher age rating than what is currently set in App Store Connect.
whatDeveloperDid: Published a card game app with simulated gambling mechanics (poker chips, betting) and cartoon combat violence, but set the age rating to 4+ in App Store Connect without properly answering the content description questionnaire.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.1 - Safety - Objectionable Content: Your app contains simulated gambling and frequent/intense cartoon violence, but the age rating is set to 4+. You must update your age rating in App Store Connect to accurately reflect the content in your app. Please review the content descriptions and adjust accordingly.
fix: Updated the App Store Connect age rating questionnaire to accurately reflect simulated gambling (selecting 'Frequent/Intense') and cartoon violence (selecting 'Frequent/Intense'), which raised the age rating to 12+.

rejectionReason: App collected children's email addresses for in-game rewards without verifiable parental consent.
whatDeveloperDid: TinyCo's kids games prompted children to enter their email addresses to receive virtual game items and rewards, collecting personal information from minors without obtaining verifiable parental consent as required by COPPA.
whatAppleSaid: This case resulted in an FTC settlement rather than a standard App Review rejection. The FTC found TinyCo violated COPPA by collecting children's email addresses without parental consent. Apple subsequently tightened enforcement of Guideline 1.3 for all Kids Category apps.
fix: TinyCo paid a $300,000 FTC settlement and removed all email collection prompts from kids games. Implemented age-gating and parental consent flows for any data collection. This case became a precedent for stricter Kids Category enforcement.
behavioralSignals: email input field in kids game

rejectionReason: Your app contains objectionable content including vulgar and profane language.
whatDeveloperDid: Ninjawords, a dictionary app, was rejected because it contained definitions for vulgar and profane words. The developer had to strip out thousands of dictionary entries that Apple deemed objectionable, even though the app was a reference dictionary.
whatAppleSaid: Your application contains content that many audiences would find objectionable. Specifically, the dictionary includes definitions for terms that are vulgar, profane, and sexually explicit in nature. You must remove this content or implement a content filter.
fix: The developer removed objectionable word definitions from the dictionary database and submitted a censored version. This case sparked public controversy about Apple's content policies, as critics argued a dictionary should be allowed to define all words. Apple later softened its stance on reference content when properly age-rated.

rejectionReason: Your app enables users to rate the physical appearance of real people, which is considered mean-spirited and objectionable.
whatDeveloperDid: Built a Hot-or-Not style app where users could upload photos of real people and other users would vote on their attractiveness with a rating system.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.1.1 - Safety - Objectionable Content: Your app allows users to rate the physical appearance of other people, which Apple considers to be mean-spirited and potentially harmful. This type of content that objectifies real individuals is not appropriate for the App Store.
fix: The concept was fundamentally incompatible with Apple's guidelines. The developer pivoted to a photo contest app where users voluntarily submitted their own photos to themed photography challenges judged on artistic merit rather than personal appearance.

rejectionReason: Your app references being designed for kids in its metadata but is not published in the Kids Category.
whatDeveloperDid: Published an app outside the Kids Category but included phrases like 'perfect for kids' and 'designed for children ages 3-8' in the app description and marketing text. The app also did not comply with Kids Category data collection restrictions.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.3 - Safety - Kids Category: Your app's metadata indicates it is designed for children, but it is not published in the Kids Category. Apps that are designed specifically for children must be published in the Kids Category and must comply with all Kids Category requirements, including restrictions on data collection, third-party analytics, and advertising.
fix: The developer had two choices: either move the app to the Kids Category and comply with all Kids Category requirements (removing third-party analytics, advertising, and external links), or remove all references to children from the app description and metadata. They chose to move to the Kids Category and strip out non-compliant SDKs.

rejectionReason: Your Kids Category app includes third-party advertising which is not permitted.
whatDeveloperDid: Published a children's coloring book app in the Kids Category that displayed AdMob banner ads and interstitial ads between activities. The ads were not age-appropriate and included tracking.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.3 - Safety - Kids Category: Your app includes third-party advertising (Google AdMob) in a Kids Category app. Apps in the Kids Category may not include third-party advertising or analytics. All ads in Kids Category apps must be appropriate for children and must not include behavioral targeting or tracking.
fix: Removed Google AdMob SDK entirely. Switched to a premium paid model with no ads. For developers who need ad revenue in Kids Category apps, Apple requires using Apple's own advertising framework (SKAdNetwork) with certified kid-safe ad content from Apple-approved networks.
behavioralSignals: behavioral ad targeting in children's app

rejectionReason: Your app with user-generated content does not include terms of service that users must agree to.
whatDeveloperDid: Built a community forum app where users could post text and images but did not include any terms of service, community guidelines, or content policy that users were required to accept before participating.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.2 - Safety - User-Generated Content: Your app enables users to share content but does not include published terms of service or community guidelines. Apps with user-generated content must include terms that clearly state what content is not acceptable and the consequences of violations.
fix: Added a Terms of Service and Community Guidelines document within the app. Implemented a mandatory acceptance flow during onboarding where users must agree to the terms before being able to post content. Added a link to the terms in the app settings.
behavioralSignals: users can post without accepting rules

rejectionReason: Your medical app does not include appropriate disclaimers about the limitations of its health-related information.
whatDeveloperDid: Created a symptom checker app that provided potential diagnoses based on user-entered symptoms without any medical disclaimers, warnings that the information is not a substitute for professional medical advice, or recommendation to consult a healthcare provider.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.4.1 - Safety - Physical Harm: Your app provides medical information and symptom analysis without appropriate disclaimers. Medical apps must clearly indicate that the information provided is not intended to replace professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. You must include prominent disclaimers throughout the app.
fix: Added a prominent disclaimer on the app's launch screen and before every symptom analysis result stating 'This app is for informational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider.' Also added a persistent disclaimer banner on all diagnosis result screens.
behavioralSignals: diagnosis results without professional consultation reminder

rejectionReason: Your app promotes tobacco and/or alcohol products without implementing an age verification gate.
whatDeveloperDid: Built a craft beer discovery app that showcased various alcohol brands, included purchasing links, and featured tobacco-related lifestyle content without any age verification or age gate on app launch.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.1.5 - Safety - Objectionable Content: Your app facilitates the sale or promotion of tobacco and alcohol products but does not include an age gate. Apps that promote or sell age-restricted products must include an appropriate age verification mechanism to ensure users meet the minimum legal age requirements.
fix: Implemented an age gate on first launch requiring users to confirm they are of legal drinking age (21+ in US). Added date-of-birth verification with persistent storage so users only need to verify once. Also added age-appropriate content warnings on alcohol-related pages.
behavioralSignals: alcohol content displayed without age check

rejectionReason: Your social media app does not include a mechanism for users to report objectionable content.
whatDeveloperDid: Launched a photo-sharing social media app with commenting and messaging features but only included content moderation on the backend. There was no user-facing way to report posts, comments, or other users for objectionable content.
whatAppleSaid: Guideline 1.2 - Safety - User-Generated Content: Your app includes user-generated content features such as photo sharing and commenting but does not provide a mechanism for users to report objectionable content and abusive users. You must implement a reporting mechanism that is easily accessible to users.
fix: Added a three-dot menu on every post and comment with a 'Report' option. Added a 'Report User' button on user profiles. Implemented a report flow with categories (spam, harassment, inappropriate content, etc.) and confirmation. Also added an in-app content moderation dashboard for the team.
